Countless speakers have been embarrassed by marching in having missed the speaker or speakers in front of them And having anything happened from literally contradicting what the person prior to them said to telling the same joke And you want to fall flat in front of an audience boy. That's the way to do it And so you'll see pros you'll see amateurs do it all the time because they're lazy So they're out in the hall They're up in a room sleeping there, you know, whatever they're doing if you watch pros Super conference Jim Rohn, I think was like he was probably in the room the whole day prior to the evening he spoke Zigg was there from the beginning McCann came down From the very beginning of the morning that he spoke For the most part It's not so much that like Jim Rohn wanted to sit in a seminar for a day At his age and experience level. He's probably done that enough He wanted to make sure that there wasn't anything going on that he was going to get in trouble with or look stupid doing When he got his two and a half hours in the evening so Just as a little side tip Because there's I find there's not much risk if you guys like modeling and copying what I do most of you aren't real good at that but But but you know It occurs to me if somebody is going to do it, they're probably going to model the thing that I showed them that they shouldn't do So just as a tip, you know, you want to you want to at least hear the guy in front of you If not a couple guys in front of you unless you are very very very certain of what they are going to do You see in Bill's case. I know there's like no risk of us telling the same Joe because He's not funny But And I'm and I'm pretty sure what the materials going to be but But otherwise, you know, you want to be up here. You want to be up here first and you want to pay attention Also, by the way, you can usually if the flip side of that is the positive is being able to build on What somebody ahead of you has said if you are speaking You don't want to derail a pat presentation that you got but you know a sentence here or reference here or reference there So if somebody has already planted certain seeds with them You know, so the Peter Low events I You know, I didn't change that presentation, but ten words in ten years But it made a difference to me if Hopkins had been on before me or if Hopkins and not had not been on before me And Tommy only did about a third of the events. I did all of them. So I didn't need to go over and hear him I know Tommy's talk But I needed to know if he was there or not because there were certain things then I didn't want to do and There were certain references I wanted to make back to what Hopkins had already said because it allowed me to build on something that had already been done by credible authority figure that they responded to and liked so If you're out speaking, you know, you you you don't want to be out in the hall kibbutz and You don't want to get there two minutes before you go on You want to pay attention to who's on the program ahead of you And if possible you at least want to hear The one who's on immediately before you So you don't do anything stupid or embarrassing and maybe you have an opportunity to do something smart When I when I used to do a lot of speaking and I used to care a lot About the results And I wasn't good at like go sit through a day if I was like an evening speaker or something I would And actually what reminded me of this is your reference to Fernandez. I mean that's 80s right PMA. Yeah, I was speaking at do a lot of speaking at PMA conferences for chiropractors Got my name in dr.
